rule: Do not use Inter as the primary typeface
why: Default-tier SaaS font; reads as 'YC startup template'. Median of training distribution.
replace_with: Geist (technical) | Outfit | Cabinet Grotesk | Satoshi | Pangram Pangram | Klim | ABC Dinamo | Velvetyne foundry

rule: Geist sans by itself reads as Vercel-default; pair with Geist Mono only for technical UIs
why: Same designer as Inter same neutral character. Becomes a tell when used alone on premium briefs.
replace_with: Geist + Geist Mono pair (technical only) | Pangram Pangram | Cabinet Grotesk | Klim | Future Fonts

rule: Never use Serif fonts on clean Dashboards
why: Serif is for editorial/creative direction only; serif-on-dashboard is a tell.
replace_with: Sans-serif (Geist Mono / Outfit / Satoshi) for dashboards. Serif only on editorial routes.

rule: H1 should not scream. Control hierarchy with weight + color not just massive scale.
why: 'text-9xl + bold' is the AI-default reach. Reduces sophistication.
replace_with: Smaller H1 (text-5xl/6xl) with deliberate weight + color contrast

rule: Never use #000000
why: Pure black reads as default/unconsidered. Off-black has more depth.

rule: Banned palette: dark navy bg + warm gold accent
why: Universal SaaS pattern (Linear Resend Vercel Stripe). Zero memorable choice.
replace_with: Force a different palette per project (Adam-named example: 'sage + clay' 'oxidised steel + paper' 'lichen + bone')

rule: Banned: #0d1117 exact GitHub default background
why: Exact GitHub default tells the viewer 'this builder didn't choose a palette'.
replace_with: Any considered dark surface that isn't GitHub-default: Zinc-950 #09090b warm-black #1a1614 etc.

rule: Banned: 4-shade-near-black surface palette (#0f → #18 → #1f → #27)
why: Universal SaaS surface stack. Defines no brand.
replace_with: Asymmetric palette: 1-2 deep tones + 1 vivid mid-tone + 1 paper/cream. Avoid monotone-near-black stacks.

rule: Do not use oversaturated accents
why: Pure HSL saturation reads as Bootstrap-template. Desaturate to elegant tonal range.
replace_with: Desaturated accents (OKLCH chroma 0.10-0.18) that read elegant against neutrals

rule: Do not default to dark mode
why: Most products are better light-first. Dark-first is an aesthetic reach used to fake sophistication.
replace_with: Choose the mode the brand wants. Light-first is the default for service/B2B; dark-first only when the brand needs it.

rule: Never auto-install a custom cursor — opt-in only
why: Custom cursors read as agency-portfolio-show-off; wrong for service-business brands. Cardinal Rule 20 demoted 2026-05-17 after Orbit Digital rejection.
replace_with: No custom cursor unless replication-mode reference uses one or Adam explicitly asks

rule: Banned: dashboard mockup in hero section
why: Stripe Linear Vercel Resend every YC SaaS uses dashboard-in-hero. Zero originality.
anti_pattern_keywords: dashboard mockup|product screenshot in hero|app preview hero
replace_with: Custom hero: WebGL letterform | scrollytelling brand frame | abstract motion piece | typographic poster | drivable 3D toy | kinetic type splitting

rule: Banned: centered headline over gradient blob with generic CTA
why: Stock-hero template. Identifies as AI-default within 1 second.
replace_with: Asymmetric hero (left-aligned text + image bleed) | editorial split | full-bleed video | non-hero ('the page IS the hero')

rule: Banned: bento grid for services/features section
why: Bento is the safest of A/B/C signatures. Picks itself when the builder has no point of view.
replace_with: Zig-zag 2-column | asymmetric overlap | sticky-rail with horizontal scroll | single-column narrative | split-screen comparison

rule: Banned: 3 equal cards horizontally for features
why: Generic feature row identifies as AI-default. Use 2-col zig-zag or asymmetric grid instead.
replace_with: 2-column zig-zag | asymmetric grid | horizontal scroll | single-column with sticky media

rule: Banned: default card grids with uniform spacing and no hierarchy
why: All cards same size + same padding + same radius = no hierarchy = template.
replace_with: Mixed-size grid with intentional asymmetry. One hero card + 2-3 smaller. Or editorial/bento with real depth.

rule: Banned: uniform radius/spacing/shadows across every component
why: All shadcn-default radii (8px everything) reads as unchanged scaffold.
replace_with: Vary radius by component role (cards larger buttons smaller chips full). Vary shadow weight by elevation tier.

rule: Banned: unmodified shadcn/Tailwind defaults passed off as finished design
why: shadcn is source-code you own. Defaults look exactly like every other shadcn product.
replace_with: Customize radii colors shadows AND component composition. Treat shadcn as a starting skeleton not a finished theme.

rule: Banned: Lucide icons inside tinted-bg squares for features
why: Universal SaaS visual library. Lucide-icon-in-rounded-square = the AI-default move.
replace_with: Custom illustration system | photographed objects | abstract geometric marks | typographic numerals | NO icons at all where copy carries the load

rule: Banned: SVG arc gauges as decoration
why: Stripe-default 'progress arc' pattern. Used everywhere as decorative fluff.
anti_pattern_keywords: arc gauge|svg arc|circular progress as decoration
replace_with: Only when the gauge is meaningfully data-driven. Decorative arcs are banned.

rule: Banned: sparklines used as decoration
why: Sparkline-as-vibe is the SaaS-aesthetic tell. OK if it shows real data.
replace_with: Only with real data series. Otherwise replace with intentional typography or imagery.

rule: Banned: workflow node diagrams as decoration
why: n8n/Zapier-default visual. Decorative when not interactive = AI-default.
anti_pattern_keywords: workflow diagram|node graph as decoration|n8n-style nodes
replace_with: Only when actually interactive/explorable. Decorative nodes are banned.

rule: Banned-by-default: GSAP pinned-scroll narrative as the signature
why: Pinned scroll is the safest of A/B/C signatures. Picks itself for risk-averse builders.
replace_with: Match motion to brand: spring physics | View Transitions API | scroll-driven CSS animations | custom canvas | NO motion (silence as choice)

rule: Never mix GSAP/ThreeJS with Framer Motion in the same component tree
why: Two motion systems fighting = visible jank. Pick one per component subtree.
replace_with: Framer Motion for UI/Bento. GSAP/ThreeJS exclusively for isolated full-page scrolltelling or canvas backgrounds with useEffect cleanup.

rule: Banned: default neon/outer glows
why: box-shadow glow is the AI-default 'futuristic' move. Looks cheap.
replace_with: Inner borders | subtle tinted shadows | layered ambient + key shadows by elevation tier

rule: Banned: text-fill gradients on large headers
why: Gradient text identifies as AI-default. Reduces legibility too.
replace_with: Solid color with weight + size hierarchy. Gradient text only when it carries brand meaning.

rule: No 'John Doe' / 'Sarah Chan' / 'Jack Su' generic names
why: Generic-name placeholders identify as AI-generated immediately.
replace_with: Highly creative realistic-sounding names ('Imogen Hartcastle' 'Renji Adekunle' 'Femke Vroom')

rule: No standard SVG egg / Lucide user-icon avatars
why: Egg avatars = AI-default placeholder.
replace_with: Creative believable photo placeholders (picsum.photos/seed/{slug}/) or specific styling

rule: No predictable round numbers (99.99% 50% 1234567)
why: Round numbers read as fabricated. Real-world data is messy.
replace_with: Organic messy data: 47.2% +1 (312) 847-1928 4.7/5 from 1.2k reviews

rule: No 'Acme' / 'Nexus' / 'SmartFlow' brand names in examples
why: Generic startup names tell viewer this is a template.
replace_with: Invent premium contextual brand names appropriate to the demo niche.

rule: Banned AI copy cliches: 'Elevate' 'Seamless' 'Unleash' 'Next-Gen' 'Empower' 'Transform' 'Streamline'
why: Identifies as AI-generated copy within one sentence.
replace_with: Concrete verbs that name the actual action ('Cut audit prep from 2 hrs to 8 min' not 'Streamline your audit workflow')

rule: No Unsplash links (they break)
why: Unsplash URLs have a history of breaking; placeholders rot.
replace_with: picsum.photos/seed/{random_string}/800/600 | self-hosted images | SVG UI Avatars

rule: Banned: safe gray-on-white styling with one accent color
why: Bootstrap-default. Defines no visual identity.
replace_with: Considered palette with 3-5 semantic roles. Use color for hierarchy not decoration.

rule: Banned: default font stacks used without deliberate reason
why: System fonts or Inter/Geist out of habit = no design decision made.
replace_with: Pick fonts deliberately. One display + one text pairing. Document the why in DESIGN-BRIEF.md.

rule: Banned: dashboard-by-numbers (sidebar + cards + charts with no point of view)
why: Visible from a thumbnail as 'every analytics product ever'.
replace_with: Pick a strong layout point of view (cockpit | editorial | bento with real depth | full-width data table with intent | dashboard split into 'now + later' frames)

rule: Banned: flat layouts with zero layering depth or motion
why: Stretches as 'minimal' but reads as 'unfinished'.
replace_with: Establish depth via overlap surfaces shadows OR motion. Pick at least one.

rule: Every project must ship one memorable choice no other site has
why: Adam's standard is Awwwards SOTM / Bruno Simon / Stripe Sessions tier — defined by ONE thing nobody else has.
replace_with: Before commit name the one thing nobody else has. Bruno Simon = drivable 3D car. Stripe Sessions = custom WebGL letterforms. Apple = letter-by-letter kinetic typography. Vercel = animated variable-axis demo native to type.

rule: Before final commit cross-check hero+services against 3 most recent projects
why: If >50% similarity restart with different direction.
anti_pattern_keywords: similar hero|similar services|same approach as last project
replace_with: Diff hero + services against 3 recent commits. If too similar pick a different signature (A/B/C — never default to safest).

rule: Production-ready code: extremely clean visually striking memorable refined
why: Code quality reads through to design quality. Sloppy code = sloppy taste.
replace_with: Lint-clean | typed end-to-end | no dead imports | code-formatted | code reviewed before commit.

rule: If using shadcn — customize radii colors shadows AND composition
why: Must look different from every other shadcn product.
replace_with: Override radii colors shadows. Compose primitives into bespoke components. Treat shadcn as a primitive library not a finished theme.
